---
title: "Ribbonwort (Pallavicinia lyellii)"
slug: "pallavicinia-lyellii"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475154108/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Ribbonwort"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Jungermanniopsida"
tax_order: "Pallaviciniales"
tax_family: "Pallaviciniaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Jungermanniopsida", "Pallaviciniales", "Pallaviciniaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475154108/medium.jpg" alt="Ribbonwort" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/ansleyburtch">
ansleyburtch</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264606704">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Jungermanniopsida
-   **Order:** Pallaviciniales
-   **Family:** Pallaviciniaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=56455)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common across Florida but restricted to high-humidity habitats.

## Natural history notes

A delicate liverwort characterized by its thin, ribbon-like green thallus that lies flat against moist surfaces. In Florida, it is found in shaded, humid habitats such as along stream banks, seepage areas, and damp soil or rock. It reproduces by spores and grows in consistently moist conditions, often forming small patches in sheltered microhabitats.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/56455-Pallavicinia-lyellii)
-   [Local profile](https://explorer.natureserve.org/Taxon/ELEMENT_GLOBAL.2.124398/Pallavicinia_lyellii)
